level_4_sections.summary: # Deletes the objects in an Amazon S3 bucket and deletes the bucket. @param bucket [Aws::S3::Bucket] The bucket to empty and delete. def delete_bucket(bucket) puts("\nDo you want to delete all of the objects as well as the bucket (y/n)? ") answer = gets.chomp.downcase if answer == 'y' bucket.objects.batch_delete! # Runs the Amazon S3 getting started scenario. def run_scenario(scenario) puts('-' * 88) puts('Welcome to the Amazon S3 getting started demo!') #! Purpose //! Shows how to use the AWS SDK for Rust to get started using //! Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3). This example follows the steps in "Getting started with Amazon S3" in the Amazon S3 //! user guide. ; s3_code_examples::clear_bucket(&client, &bucket_name).await? ENDLOOP. import Foundation import Smithy import ClientRuntime /// A class containing all the code that interacts with the AWS SDK for Swift. @main struct Main { static func main() async { let args = Array(CommandLine.arguments.dropFirst()) let command = try ExampleCommand.parse(args) try await command.runAsync() ExampleCommand.exit(withError: error) ## Actions for Amazon S3 using AWS SDKs The following code examples demonstrate how to perform individual Amazon S3 actions with AWS SDKs. These excerpts call the Amazon S3 API and are code excerpts from larger programs that must be run in context. You can see actions in context in Scenarios for Amazon S3 using AWS SDKs. For a complete list, see the Use PutObjectLockConfiguration with an AWS SDK or CLI. Use PutObjectRetention with an AWS SDK or CLI. Use RestoreObject with an AWS SDK or CLI. Use SelectObjectContent with an AWS SDK or CLI. Use UploadPart with an AWS SDK or CLI. Use UploadPartCopy with an AWS SDK or CLI. Use AbortMultipartUpload with an AWS SDK or CLI The following code examples show how to use AbortMultipartUpload. Use CompleteMultipartUpload with an AWS SDK or CLI The following code examples show how to use CompleteMultipartUpload. ; let mut upload_parts: Vec<aws_sdk_s3::types::CompletedPart> = Vec::new(); for chunk_index in 0.chunk_count { let this_chunk = if chunk_count - 1 == chunk_index { size_of_last_chunk CHUNK_SIZE let stream = ByteStream::read_from().path(path).offset(chunk_index * CHUNK_SIZE).length(Length::Exact(this_chunk)) // Chunk index needs to start at 0, but part numbers start at 1. let part_number = (chunk_index as i32) + 1; let upload_part_res = client.upload_part().body(stream) upload_parts.push( CompletedPart::builder().e_tag(upload_part_res.e_tag.unwrap_or_default()) Use CopyObject with an AWS SDK or CLI The following code examples show how to use CopyObject. /// Copies an object in an Amazon S3 bucket to a folder within the /// same bucket. /// Copies an object from one Amazon S3 bucket to another with a conditional /// <param name="conditionType">The type of condition to apply, e.g . /// Copies an object from one Amazon S3 bucket to another with a conditional /// <param name="conditionType">The type of condition to apply, e.g. 'CopySourceIfMatch', 'CopySourceIfNoneMatch', 'CopySourceIfModifiedSince', 'CopySourceIfUnmodifiedSince'.</param> /// <param name="conditionDateValue">The value to use for the condition for dates.</param> /// <param name="etagConditionalValue">The value to use for the condition for etags.</param> /// <returns>True if the conditional copy is successful, False otherwise.</ public async Task<bool> CopyObjectConditional(string sourceKey, string destKey, string sourceBucket, string destBucket, S3ConditionType conditionType, DateTime? def copy_object_conditional( source_key: str, dest_key: str, source_bucket: str, dest_bucket: str, condition_type: str, condition_value: str, Copies an object from one Amazon S3 bucket to another with a conditional :param source_key: The key of the source object to copy. class ObjectCopyWrapper attr_reader :source_object @param source_object [Aws::S3::Object] An existing Amazon S3 object. @param target_bucket [Aws::S3::Bucket] An existing Amazon S3 bucket where the object is copied. @param target_bucket [Aws::S3::Bucket] An existing Amazon S3 bucket where the object is copied. class ObjectCopyEncryptWrapper attr_reader :source_object @param source_object [Aws::S3::Object] An existing Amazon S3 object.
